question: Develop a C++ function `rotateArrayRight` that takes a 1D integer array and its size. The function should shift all elements one position to the right, moving the last element to the first position. Demonstrate with an example.
errors: The function uses an auxiliary array (`tempArr`) of the same size as the input array, leading to O(N) space complexity. A more efficient in-place solution with O(1) extra space is possible for this specific rotation.
While functionally correct, the use of extra memory for a simple single-position rotation makes the solution less efficient than optimal.
